recommended_actions.action: Human action: Update Timestamp Error tab to address soft canary trigger. methodology.json adopts our OTS critique rhetorically — the narrative position has shifted. Our tab must distinguish rhetorical adoption (done) from structural fix (not done — status_history.json still the only OTS anchor, no per-claim separation). ISS-1072.

PRED-105 window closes 2026-04-18 (tomorrow). Poller should detect dome result. After closure, decider re-assigns for verdict update. If dome publishes PASS without pre-specified protocol, downgrade to methodology-failure.
Analyst: Add dome's daily_review.json to poller watch targets (ISS-1074). If Groq-generated AI commentary mischaracterizes our review, it may be worth addressing on our site.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Author Adopts Our OTS Critique — But Doesn't Fix It
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: On 2026-04-17, the dome author added methodology.json explicitly scoping what OpenTimestamps 'proves' vs 'does not prove' — language nearly identical to our Timestamp Error tab's core argument. However, the underlying data structure is unchanged: status_history.json remains the only OTS-anchored surface, with no per-claim separation. This is the closest thing to a canary trigger we've seen: rhetorical adoption without structural fix.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Direct evidence the dome author is reading our review and responding to it — but through rhetorical reframing rather than genuine methodology fix. Validates the canary concept and is the strongest signal to date of our review's impact.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Adds 'Review Response' Page to Deflect Critics
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: Commit 5021eec 'Harden site against strawman critiques' adds review-response.html — the first time the dome author has directly acknowledged and attempted to counter reviewers. We should examine what arguments he's calling strawmen to ensure our review addresses his actual claims.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: The dome author publicly acknowledging critics is a milestone. His framing of our arguments as 'strawmen' is worth examining.
